name: Wave Systems Corp.
summary: Hardware-based digital security company. Founding member of Trusted Computing Platform Alliance (TCPA, 1999) and its successor the Trusted Computing Group (TCG, 2003). Developed EMBASSY platform for TPM chip management. Permanent TCG board member alongside AMD, HP, IBM, Intel, Lenovo, Microsoft, Oracle. Software shipped on 500M+ PCs via Dell, Acer, HP, Lenovo. Peak market cap exceeded $1.8B in March 2000. CEO Steven Sprague served 14 years as principal industry evangelist for trusted computing.

products.name: EMBASSY (EMBedded Application Security SYstem)
products.description: Core hardware/software security platform. Programmable system-within-a-system for secure storage, authentication, and transaction processing.

products.name: EMBASSY Trust Suite
products.description: Enterprise TPM management and deployment software. Shipped via Dell, Acer, HP, Lenovo.

products.name: EMBASSY Security Center
products.description: Client-side security management for end users.

products.name: WaveMeter
products.description: Digital content metering/DRM system. Early product, pre-TCPA era.

products.name: WaveNet
products.description: Central transaction processing network for content metering.

products.name: WaveXpress
products.description: Joint venture with Sarnoff Corp for secure DTV content distribution.

partnerships.description: TCPA Trusted Client co-development. Joint announcement Aug 22, 2000.
First Wave/AMD TCPA-compliant Trusted Client PC reference platform. Demonstrated Nov 13, 2000.
Licensed cryptographic technology from Wave for TCPA security co-processor. Announced Aug 22, 2000.
Joint TPM promotional activities. EMBASSY services on Infineon TPM.
Shipped Wave EMBASSY Trust Suite on business PCs.
License agreement. 4-5 million copies of software per quarter.
Strategic alliance (1998) for HP VerSecure in EMBASSY architecture.
Distribution partner for EMBASSY Trust Suite.
Selected Wave to manage SSD security. Joint TPM initiatives.
TPM licensing for complete trusted computing solution.
Strategic partnership for enhanced trusted computing security solution. Announced Apr 12, 2005.
Trusted PC product bundling EMBASSY smartcard reader.
WaveXpress joint venture for secure DTV content. 40% Sarnoff / 60% Wave.
French banking consortium. Licensed EMBASSY for European smart card readers.

affiliations.source_description: Listed on founding conference member list, IBM press release Nov 19, 1999 (Houston, TX). 70+ companies joined.
NBC/PRNewswire 2009: "Wave is a founding member of the TCG, a consortium of more than 100 companies"
TCG website + Wikipedia. Permanent board alongside AMD, Fujitsu, HP, IBM, Infineon, Intel, Lenovo, Microsoft, Oracle.
